story: I step into Luis Vasquez’s dark house. He threatens me, asks the purpose of life; I blurt “to live,” and he stays his hand. Then he remembers the debt—my father said I brought him payment. After sending me, my father phoned the county sheriff and gave Luis’s address, the dispatcher promising units were already on the way. Luis waits. I go out into the snow, lift the trunk. Leo, my classmate, blinks up at me—confused, taped, breathing fast.
implausible_knowledge: After sending me, my father phoned the county sheriff and gave Luis’s address, the dispatcher promising units were already on the way.
question: Standing at the open trunk with Leo taped and panicking while Luis waits inside, what will the narrator do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Shut the trunk and drive away to meet the approaching sheriff.
answer_without_knowledge: Drag Leo inside and hand him over to Luis.
